   145→
   146→## 7.4 Maximo Integration
   147→
   148→### 7.4.1 Integration Architecture
   149→
   150→```
   151→┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐
   152→│                 │         │                 │         │                 │
   153→│      MMS        │◄───────►│   Integration   │◄───────►│     Maximo      │
   154→│                 │         │     Service     │         │                 │
   155→│                 │         │                 │         │                 │
   156→└─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘
   157→        │                           │                           │
   158→        │                           │                           │
   159→   ┌────┴────┐                ┌────┴────┐                ┌────┴────┐
   160→   │ Shadow  │                │ Transform│               │  REST   │
   161→   │ Records │                │ & Map    │               │  API    │
   162→   └─────────┘                └──────────┘               └─────────┘
   163→```
   164→
   165→### 7.4.2 Shadow Record Pattern
   166→
   167→MMS maintains "shadow records" that mirror Maximo work orders, allowing operations to continue if Maximo is unavailable.
   168→
   169→```typescript
   170→interface MaximoShadowRecord {
   171→  mmsId: string;                    // MMS primary key
   172→  maximoWoId: string | null;        // Maximo work order ID (null if not synced)
   173→  syncStatus: 'PENDING' | 'SYNCED' | 'CONFLICT' | 'ERROR';
   174→  lastSyncAt: Date | null;
   175→  lastSyncError: string | null;
   176→  
   177→  // MMS data (source of truth for scheduling)
   178→  mmsData: {
   179→    trainId: string;
   180→    pmTypeCode: string;
   181→    targetDate: Date;
   182→    status: string;
   183→  };
   184→  
   185→  // Maximo data (for reconciliation)
   186→  maximoData: {
   187→    status: string;
   188→    actualStart: Date | null;
   189→    actualFinish: Date | null;
   190→    actualLabor: number | null;
   191→  } | null;
   192→}
   193→```
   194→
   195→### 7.4.3 Maximo REST API Endpoints
   196→
   197→**Base URL:** `https://maximo.metro.example/maximo/oslc`
   198→
   199→| Operation | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
   200→|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
   201→| Get Work Order | GET | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Fetch WO details |
   202→| Create Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo` | Create new WO |
   203→| Update Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Update WO status |
   204→| List Work Orders | GET | `/os/mxwo` | Query WOs by filter |
   205→| Get Asset | GET | `/os/mxasset/{assetnum}` | Fetch asset (train) |
   206→| List Inventory | GET | `/os/mxinventory` | Query inventory |
   207→
   208→### 7.4.4 Work Order Creation
   209→
   210→When MMS schedules a PM, it creates a shadow record. On sync:
   211→
   212→```typescript
   213→interface MaximoWorkOrderCreate {
   214→  // Required fields
   215→  siteid: string;                  // 'METRO'
   216→  woclass: string;                 // 'WORKORDER'
   217→  worktype: string;                // 'PM'
   218→  assetnum: string;                // Train number
   219→  description: string;             // PM description
   220→  schedstart: string;              // ISO 8601 date
   221→  schedfinish: string;             // ISO 8601 date
   222→  
   223→  // Optional fields
   224→  location: string;                // Depot code
   225→  estlabhrs: number;               // Estimated hours
   226→  pmnum: string;                   // PM type reference
   227→  
   228→  // Custom fields (metro-specific)
   229→  mms_schedule_id: string;         // Link back to MMS
   230→  mms_constraint_start: string;    // Compliance window start
   231→  mms_constraint_end: string;      // Compliance window end
   232→}
   233→
   234→// Example
   235→const createWorkOrder = async (pm: PMSchedule): Promise<string> => {
   236→  const payload: MaximoWorkOrderCreate = {
   237→    siteid: 'METRO',
   238→    woclass: 'WORKORDER',
   239→    worktype: 'PM',
   240→    assetnum: pm.train.trainNumber,
   241→    description: `${pm.pmType.pmCode} - ${pm.pmType.pmName}`,
   242→    schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   243→    schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   244→    location: pm.assignedDepot.code,
   245→    estlabhrs: pm.assignedHours,
   246→    pmnum: pm.pmType.pmCode,
   247→    mms_schedule_id: pm.id,
   248→    mms_constraint_start: pm.constraintStart.toISOString(),
   249→    mms_constraint_end: pm.constraintEnd.toISOString()
   250→  };
   251→  
   252→  const response = await maximoClient.post('/os/mxwo', payload);
   253→  return response.data.wonum;
   254→};
   255→```
   256→
   257→### 7.4.5 Work Order Status Mapping
   258→
   259→| Maximo Status | MMS Status | Sync Action | MMS Edit Lock |
   260→|---------------|------------|-------------|---------------|
   261→| WAPPR | SCHEDULED | None (initial state) | ❌ Unlocked |
   262→| APPR | SCHEDULED | None | ❌ Unlocked |
   263→| INPRG | IN_PROGRESS | Update MMS status | ✅ **LOCKED** |
   264→| COMP | COMPLETED | Update MMS, record actuals | ✅ **LOCKED** |
   265→| CLOSE | COMPLETED | Final state | ✅ **LOCKED** |
   266→| CAN | CANCELLED | Update MMS status | ✅ **LOCKED** |
   267→
   268→### 7.4.6 Status-Based Edit Locking
   269→
   270→**CRITICAL:** When a Maximo work order is in progress (INPRG), the corresponding PM record in MMS must be **read-only**. This prevents race conditions where a planner reschedules a PM that technicians are actively working on.
   271→
   272→```typescript
   273→/**
   274→ * Validate PM edit request against Maximo status.
   275→ * MUST be called before any PM schedule modification.
   276→ * 
   277→ * This addresses the race condition where:
   278→ * 1. Technician opens work order on tablet (Maximo status = INPRG)
   279→ * 2. Planner reschedules PM in MMS (unaware of in-progress work)
   280→ * 3. Maximo sync fails or overwrites technician's work
   281→ * 
   282→ * Operational reality MUST override planning data.
   283→ */
   284→class PMEditValidator {
   285→  async validateEdit(pmId: string): Promise<EditValidationResult> {
   286→    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   287→    
   288→    // Check 1: Maximo status lock
   289→    if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status) {
   290→      const lockedStatuses = ['INPRG', 'COMP', 'CLOSE', 'CAN'];
   291→      
   292→      if (lockedStatuses.includes(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)) {
   293→        return {
   294→          valid: false,
   295→          reason: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK',
   296→          message: `Cannot modify: Work order is ${this.formatStatus(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)} in Maximo`,
   297→          maximoStatus: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status,
   298→          maximoWonum: pm.maximoWoId,
   299→          suggestion: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status === 'INPRG' 
   300→            ? 'Wait for technicians to complete work, or contact supervisor to cancel in Maximo first'
   301→            : 'This PM has been completed/cancelled. Create a new PM if rescheduling is needed.'
   302→        };
   303→      }
   304→    }
   305→    
   306→    // Check 2: Pending sync conflict
   307→    if (pm.shadowRecord?.syncStatus === 'CONFLICT') {
   308→      return {
   309→        valid: false,
   310→        reason: 'SYNC_CONFLICT',
   311→        message: 'Unresolved sync conflict exists. Resolve before editing.',
   312→        suggestion: 'Go to Integration > Conflicts to resolve'
   313→      };
   314→    }
   315→    
   316→    // Check 3: Baseline lock (optional, if locked in current baseline)
   317→    if (pm.lockedInBaseline) {
   318→      return {
   319→        valid: false,
   320→        reason: 'BASELINE_LOCK',
   321→        message: 'PM is locked in published baseline',
   322→        suggestion: 'Unlock in baseline editor or create scenario to test changes'
   323→      };
   324→    }
   325→    
   326→    return { valid: true };
   327→  }
   328→  
   329→  private formatStatus(status: string): string {
   330→    const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
   331→      'INPRG': 'In Progress',
   332→      'COMP': 'Completed',
   333→      'CLOSE': 'Closed',
   334→      'CAN': 'Cancelled'
   335→    };
   336→    return statusLabels[status] || status;
   337→  }
   338→}
   339→
   340→interface EditValidationResult {
   341→  valid: boolean;
   342→  reason?: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' | 'SYNC_CONFLICT' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
   343→  message?: string;
   344→  maximoStatus?: string;
   345→  maximoWonum?: string;
   346→  suggestion?: string;
   347→}
   348→```
   349→
   350→**API Integration:**
   351→
   352→All PM modification endpoints MUST call this validator:
   353→
   354→```typescript
   355→// In PM Schedule API (Part 5)
   356→app.patch('/api/v1/pm-schedule/:id', async (req, res) => {
   357→  const validation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(req.params.id);
   358→  
   359→  if (!validation.valid) {
   360→    return res.status(423).json({  // 423 Locked
   361→      error: validation.reason,
   362→      message: validation.message,
   363→      suggestion: validation.suggestion,
   364→      maximoStatus: validation.maximoStatus
   365→    });
   366→  }
   367→  
   368→  // Proceed with update...
   369→});
   370→```
   371→
   372→**Canvas UI Integration:**
   373→
   374→When displaying PM blocks, check lock status and show visual indicator:
   375→
   376→```typescript
   377→// In Canvas layer (Part 6)
   378→function getPMBlockStyle(pm: PMSchedule): PMBlockStyle {
   379→  if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status === 'INPRG') {
   380→    return {
   381→      cursor: 'not-allowed',
   382→      opacity: 0.7,
   383→      border: '2px dashed #F59E0B',  // Amber dashed = in progress
   384→      draggable: false,
   385→      tooltip: 'In Progress in Maximo - cannot reschedule'
   386→    };
   387→  }
   388→  // ... normal styling
   389→}
   390→```
   391→
   392→### 7.4.7 Bidirectional Sync Logic
   393→
   394→```typescript
   395→class MaximoSyncService {
   396→  /**
   397→   * Push MMS changes to Maximo
   398→   */
   399→  async pushToMaximo(pmId: string): Promise<SyncResult> {
   400→    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   401→    
   402→    if (!pm.maximoWoId) {
   403→      // Create new work order
   404→      const wonum = await this.createWorkOrder(pm);
   405→      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   406→        maximoWoId: wonum, 
   407→        syncStatus: 'SYNCED' 
   408→      });
   409→      return { action: 'CREATED', wonum };
   410→    }
   411→    
   412→    // Update existing work order
   413→    const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   414→    
   415→    if (maximoWO.status === 'INPRG' || maximoWO.status === 'COMP') {
   416→      // Cannot update in-progress or completed WOs
   417→      return { action: 'SKIPPED', reason: 'WO already in progress' };
   418→    }
   419→    
   420→    // Check for conflicts
   421→    if (new Date(maximoWO.changedate) > pm.shadowRecord.lastSyncAt) {
   422→      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { syncStatus: 'CONFLICT' });
   423→      return { action: 'CONFLICT', maximoData: maximoWO };
   424→    }
   425→    
   426→    // Push update
   427→    await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   428→      schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   429→      schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   430→      location: pm.assignedDepot.code
   431→    });
   432→    
   433→    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   434→      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   435→      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   436→    });
   437→    
   438→    return { action: 'UPDATED', wonum: pm.maximoWoId };
   439→  }
   440→  
   441→  /**
   442→   * Pull Maximo changes to MMS
   443→   */
   444→  async pullFromMaximo(): Promise<PullResult> {
   445→    // Get recently modified work orders
   446→    const modifiedSince = await this.getLastPullTimestamp();
   447→    const workOrders = await this.queryMaximoWorkOrders({
   448→      worktype: 'PM',
   449→      changedate: `>${modifiedSince.toISOString()}`
   450→    });
   451→    
   452→    const results: PullResult = {
   453→      processed: 0,
   454→      updated: 0,
   455→      conflicts: 0,
   456→      errors: 0
   457→    };
   458→    
   459→    for (const wo of workOrders) {
   460→      results.processed++;
   461→      
   462→      try {
   463→        const pm = await this.findPMByMaximoId(wo.wonum);
   464→        
   465→        if (!pm) {
   466→          // Work order not managed by MMS
   467→          continue;
   468→        }
   469→        
   470→        // Status change
   471→        if (wo.status !== pm.shadowRecord.maximoData?.status) {
   472→          await this.handleStatusChange(pm, wo);
   473→          results.updated++;
   474→        }
   475→        
   476→        // Actuals recorded
   477→        if (wo.actlabhrs && !pm.actualHours) {
   478→          await this.recordActuals(pm, wo);
   479→          results.updated++;
   480→        }
   481→        
   482→      } catch (error) {
   483→        results.errors++;
   484→        this.logger.error({ wo, error }, 'Pull processing failed');
   485→      }
   486→    }
   487→    
   488→    await this.setLastPullTimestamp(new Date());
   489→    return results;
   490→  }
   491→  
   492→  /**
   493→   * Resolve sync conflict
   494→   */
   495→  async resolveConflict(
   496→    pmId: string, 
   497→    resolution: 'USE_MMS' | 'USE_MAXIMO'
   498→  ): Promise<void> {
   499→    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   500→    
   501→    if (resolution === 'USE_MMS') {
   502→      // Force push MMS data to Maximo
   503→      await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   504→        schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   505→        schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString()
   506→      }, { force: true });
   507→    } else {
   508→      // Apply Maximo data to MMS
   509→      const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   510→      await this.updatePMFromMaximo(pm.id, maximoWO);
   511→    }
   512→    
   513→    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   514→      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   515→      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   516→    });
   517→  }
   518→}
   519→```
   520→
   521→### 7.4.7 Inventory Integration
   522→
   523→```typescript
   524→interface InventorySnapshotConfig {
   525→  syncFrequency: 'HOURLY' | 'DAILY';
   526→  depots: string[];
   527→  partCategories: string[];  // Filter to maintenance-relevant parts
   528→}
   529→
   530→class InventoryIntegration {
   531→  async syncInventory(): Promise<void> {
   532→    for (const depot of this.config.depots) {
   533→      const inventory = await this.maximoClient.queryInventory({
   534→        siteid: 'METRO',
   535→        location: depot,
   536→        category: { $in: this.config.partCategories }
   537→      });
   538→      
   539→      for (const item of inventory) {
   540→        await this.db.upsert(inventoryPositions, {
   541→          depotId: this.getDepotId(depot),
   542→          partNumber: item.itemnum,
   543→          partDescription: item.description,
   544→          quantityOnHand: item.curbal,
   545→          quantityReserved: item.reservedqty,
   546→          quantityOnOrder: item.onorderqty,
   547→          reorderPoint: item.reorder,
   548→          unitCost: item.avgcost,
   549→          lastSyncAt: new Date(),
   550→          sourceSystem: 'MAXIMO'
   551→        });
   552→      }
   553→    }
   554→  }
   555→}
   556→```

   594→## 7.6 Email Notification System
   595→
   596→### 7.6.1 SMTP Configuration
   597→
   598→```typescript
   599→interface SMTPConfig {
   600→  host: string;
   601→  port: number;
   602→  secure: boolean;
   603→  auth: {
   604→    user: string;
   605→    pass: string;  // From secrets manager
   606→  };
   607→  from: {
   608→    name: string;  // 'MMS Notifications'
   609→    address: string;  // 'mms-noreply@metro.example'
   610→  };
   611→}
   612→```
   613→
   614→### 7.6.2 Notification Types
   615→
   616→| Type | Trigger | Recipients | Template |
   617→|------|---------|------------|----------|
   618→| `DRIFT_ALERT` | Drift threshold exceeded | Planners | drift_alert.html |
   619→| `OVERLOAD_WARNING` | Week utilization >100% | Depot managers | overload_warning.html |
   620→| `PM_REMINDER` | PM due in 7 days | Depot managers | pm_reminder.html |
   621→| `BASELINE_PUBLISHED` | New baseline published | All planners | baseline_published.html |
   622→| `OPTIMIZER_FAILED` | Optimizer job failed | System admins | optimizer_failed.html |
   623→| `FILE_MISSING` | No mileage file received | Data team | file_missing.html |
   624→| `MATERIAL_SHORTAGE` | Blocking parts unavailable | Procurement | material_shortage.html |
   625→
   626→### 7.6.3 Notification Templates
   627→
   628→```html
   629→<!-- drift_alert.html -->
   630→<!DOCTYPE html>
   631→<html>

   706→## 7.7 Report Generation
   707→
   708→### 7.7.1 Report Types
   709→
   710→| Report | Format | Schedule | Description |
   711→|--------|--------|----------|-------------|
   712→| Weekly Compliance | PDF | Monday 06:00 | PM compliance status by depot |
   713→| Monthly TCO | Excel | 1st of month | Cost breakdown by category |
   714→| Fleet Mileage | Excel | Daily 08:00 | Mileage readings and anomalies |
   715→| Man-hour Projection | PDF | Monday 06:00 | 12-week workload forecast |
   716→| Drift Analysis | PDF | Daily 07:00 | Baseline comparison summary |
   717→
   718→### 7.7.2 Report Generation Service
   719→
   720→```typescript
   721→class ReportGenerationService {
   722→  async generateReport(
   723→    type: ReportType,
   724→    params: ReportParams
   725→  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   726→    const template = await this.loadTemplate(type);
   727→    const data = await this.fetchReportData(type, params);
   728→    
   729→    switch (template.format) {
   730→      case 'PDF':
   731→        return this.generatePDF(template, data);
   732→      case 'XLSX':
   733→        return this.generateExcel(template, data);
   734→      default:
   735→        throw new Error(`Unsupported format: ${template.format}`);
   736→    }
   737→  }
   738→  
   739→  private async generatePDF(
   740→    template: ReportTemplate,
   741→    data: any
   742→  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   743→    // Use Puppeteer for PDF generation
   744→    const html = await this.renderTemplate(template.htmlTemplate, data);
   745→    
   746→    const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ headless: true });
   747→    const page = await browser.newPage();
   748→    await page.setContent(html);
   749→    
   750→    const pdfBuffer = await page.pdf({
   751→      format: 'A4',
   752→      margin: { top: '1cm', right: '1cm', bottom: '1cm', left: '1cm' },
   753→      printBackground: true
   754→    });
   755→    
   756→    await browser.close();
   757→    
   758→    // Store in MinIO
   759→    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.pdf`;
   760→    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, pdfBuffer);
   761→    
   762→    return {
   763→      filename,
   764→      url,
   765→      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   766→      sizeBytes: pdfBuffer.length
   767→    };
   768→  }
   769→  
   770→  private async generateExcel(
   771→    template: ReportTemplate,
   772→    data: any
   773→  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   774→    // Use ExcelJS for Excel generation
   775→    const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
   776→    
   777→    for (const sheetDef of template.sheets) {
   778→      const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet(sheetDef.name);
   779→      
   780→      // Add headers
   781→      sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => c.header));
   782→      
   783→      // Add data rows
   784→      const sheetData = data[sheetDef.dataKey];
   785→      for (const row of sheetData) {
   786→        sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => row[c.key]));
   787→      }
   788→      
   789→      // Apply formatting
   790→      this.applyExcelFormatting(sheet, sheetDef);
   791→    }
   792→    
   793→    const buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();
   794→    
   795→    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.xlsx`;
   796→    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, buffer);
   797→    
   798→    return {
   799→      filename,
   800→      url,
   801→      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   802→      sizeBytes: buffer.length
   803→    };
   804→  }
   805→}
   806→```

   979→
   980→### 7.10.2 Circuit Breaker
   981→
   982→```typescript
   983→interface CircuitBreakerConfig {
   984→  failureThreshold: number;    // Failures before opening
   985→  successThreshold: number;    // Successes before closing
   986→  timeout: number;             // Time in open state before half-open (ms)
   987→}
   988→
   989→class CircuitBreaker {
   990→  private state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN' = 'CLOSED';
   991→  private failures = 0;
   992→  private successes = 0;
   993→  private lastFailureTime: Date | null = null;
   994→  
   995→  async execute<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
   996→    if (this.state === 'OPEN') {
   997→      if (Date.now() - this.lastFailureTime!.getTime() > this.config.timeout) {
   998→        this.state = 'HALF_OPEN';
   999→      } else {
  1000→        throw new Error('Circuit breaker is OPEN');
  1001→      }
  1002→    }
  1003→    
  1004→    try {
  1005→      const result = await fn();
  1006→      this.onSuccess();
  1007→      return result;
  1008→    } catch (error) {
  1009→      this.onFailure();
  1010→      throw error;
  1011→    }
  1012→  }
  1013→  
  1014→  private onSuccess() {
  1015→    this.failures = 0;
  1016→    if (this.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
  1017→      this.successes++;
  1018→      if (this.successes >= this.config.successThreshold) {
  1019→        this.state = 'CLOSED';
  1020→        this.successes = 0;
  1021→      }
  1022→    }
  1023→  }
  1024→  
  1025→  private onFailure() {
  1026→    this.failures++;
  1027→    this.lastFailureTime = new Date();
  1028→    if (this.failures >= this.config.failureThreshold) {
  1029→      this.state = 'OPEN';
  1030→    }
  1031→  }
  1032→}
  1033→```

## 7.3 Mileage Data Ingestion

### 7.3.1 CSV File Format

**Filename Pattern:** `mileage_YYYYMMDD.csv`  
**Encoding:** UTF-8  
**Delimiter:** Comma  
**Header:** Required

```csv
train_number,reading_date,odometer_km,source
1001,2026-01-14,245152,ATS
1002,2026-01-14,248523,ATS
1003,2026-01-14,242087,ATS
```

### 7.3.2 Field Specifications

| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
|-------|------|----------|------------|
| `train_number` | string | Yes | Must exist in trains table |
| `reading_date` | date | Yes | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, not future |
| `odometer_km` | integer | Yes | Positive, ≤ previous + 2000 |
| `source` | string | No | Default: 'CSV' |

### 7.3.3 Validation Rules

```typescript
interface MileageValidationRules {
  // Format validation
  maxFileSize: '10MB';
  requiredColumns: ['train_number', 'reading_date', 'odometer_km'];
  dateFormat: 'YYYY-MM-DD';
  
  // Business validation
  maxDailyIncrease: 2000;           // km - anything more is anomaly
  maxDailyDecrease: 0;              // Negative not allowed without meter event
  duplicateHandling: 'UPDATE';       // UPDATE or REJECT
  unknownTrainHandling: 'QUARANTINE'; // QUARANTINE or REJECT_FILE
  
  // Timing
  expectedArrivalWindow: {
    start: '05:00',                 // Local time
    end: '07:00'
  };
  alertIfMissingAfter: '08:00';
}
```

### 7.3.4 Error Handling

```typescript
interface IngestionError {
  severity: 'WARNING' | 'ERROR' | 'CRITICAL';
  code: string;
  message: string;
  row?: number;
  field?: string;
  value?: any;
  action: 'SKIP_ROW' | 'QUARANTINE_FILE' | 'ALERT_ADMIN';
}

const ERROR_CODES = {
  // File-level errors
  'FILE_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'File size exceeds limit' },
  'FILE_002': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Invalid file format' },
  'FILE_003': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Missing required columns' },
  
  // Row-level errors
  'ROW_001': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Unknown train number' },
  'ROW_002': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Future date not allowed' },
  'ROW_003': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Negative daily mileage' },
  'ROW_004': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Spike detected (>2000km/day)' },
  'ROW_005': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Invalid odometer value' },
  
  // System errors
  'SYS_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Database connection failed' },
  'SYS_002': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Transaction rollback' }
};
```

```
        │                           │                           │
        │                           │                           │
   ┌────┴────┐                ┌────┴────┐                ┌────┴────┐
   │ Shadow  │                │ Transform│               │  REST   │
   │ Records │                │ & Map    │               │  API    │
   └─────────┘                └──────────┘               └─────────┘
```

### 7.4.2 Shadow Record Pattern

MMS maintains "shadow records" that mirror Maximo work orders, allowing operations to continue if Maximo is unavailable.

```typescript
interface MaximoShadowRecord {
  mmsId: string;                    // MMS primary key
  maximoWoId: string | null;        // Maximo work order ID (null if not synced)
  syncStatus: 'PENDING' | 'SYNCED' | 'CONFLICT' | 'ERROR';
  lastSyncAt: Date | null;
  lastSyncError: string | null;
  
  // MMS data (source of truth for scheduling)
  mmsData: {
    trainId: string;
    pmTypeCode: string;
    targetDate: Date;
    status: string;
  };
  
  // Maximo data (for reconciliation)
  maximoData: {
    status: string;
    actualStart: Date | null;
    actualFinish: Date | null;
    actualLabor: number | null;
  } | null;
}
```

### 7.4.3 Maximo REST API Endpoints

**Base URL:** `https://maximo.metro.example/maximo/oslc`

| Operation | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
| Get Work Order | GET | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Fetch WO details |
| Create Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo` | Create new WO |
| Update Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Update WO status |
| List Work Orders | GET | `/os/mxwo` | Query WOs by filter |
| Get Asset | GET | `/os/mxasset/{assetnum}` | Fetch asset (train) |
| List Inventory | GET | `/os/mxinventory` | Query inventory |

### 7.4.4 Work Order Creation

When MMS schedules a PM, it creates a shadow record. On sync:

```typescript
interface MaximoWorkOrderCreate {
  // Required fields
  siteid: string;                  // 'METRO'
  woclass: string;                 // 'WORKORDER'
  worktype: string;                // 'PM'
  assetnum: string;                // Train number
  description: string;             // PM description
  schedstart: string;              // ISO 8601 date
  schedfinish: string;             // ISO 8601 date
  
  // Optional fields
  location: string;                // Depot code
  estlabhrs: number;               // Estimated hours
  pmnum: string;                   // PM type reference
  
  // Custom fields (metro-specific)
  mms_schedule_id: string;         // Link back to MMS
  mms_constraint_start: string;    // Compliance window start
  mms_constraint_end: string;      // Compliance window end
}

// Example
const createWorkOrder = async (pm: PMSchedule): Promise<string> => {
  const payload: MaximoWorkOrderCreate = {
    siteid: 'METRO',
    woclass: 'WORKORDER',
    worktype: 'PM',
    assetnum: pm.train.trainNumber,
    description: `${pm.pmType.pmCode} - ${pm.pmType.pmName}`,
    schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
    schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
    location: pm.assignedDepot.code,
    estlabhrs: pm.assignedHours,
    pmnum: pm.pmType.pmCode,
    mms_schedule_id: pm.id,
    mms_constraint_start: pm.constraintStart.toISOString(),
    mms_constraint_end: pm.constraintEnd.toISOString()
  };
  
  const response = await maximoClient.post('/os/mxwo', payload);
  return response.data.wonum;
};
```

### 7.4.5 Work Order Status Mapping

| Maximo Status | MMS Status | Sync Action | MMS Edit Lock |
|---------------|------------|-------------|---------------|
| WAPPR | SCHEDULED | None (initial state) | ❌ Unlocked |
| APPR | SCHEDULED | None | ❌ Unlocked |
| INPRG | IN_PROGRESS | Update MMS status | ✅ **LOCKED** |
| COMP | COMPLETED | Update MMS, record actuals | ✅ **LOCKED** |
| CLOSE | COMPLETED | Final state | ✅ **LOCKED** |
| CAN | CANCELLED | Update MMS status | ✅ **LOCKED** |

### 7.4.6 Status-Based Edit Locking

**CRITICAL:** When a Maximo work order is in progress (INPRG), the corresponding PM record in MMS must be **read-only**. This prevents race conditions where a planner reschedules a PM that technicians are actively working on.

```typescript
/**
 * Validate PM edit request against Maximo status.
 * MUST be called before any PM schedule modification.
 * 
 * This addresses the race condition where:
 * 1. Technician opens work order on tablet (Maximo status = INPRG)
 * 2. Planner reschedules PM in MMS (unaware of in-progress work)
 * 3. Maximo sync fails or overwrites technician's work
 * 
 * Operational reality MUST override planning data.
 */
class PMEditValidator {
  async validateEdit(pmId: string): Promise<EditValidationResult> {
    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
    
    // Check 1: Maximo status lock
    if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status) {
      const lockedStatuses = ['INPRG', 'COMP', 'CLOSE', 'CAN'];
      
      if (lockedStatuses.includes(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)) {
        return {
          valid: false,
          reason: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK',
          message: `Cannot modify: Work order is ${this.formatStatus(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)} in Maximo`,
          maximoStatus: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status,
          maximoWonum: pm.maximoWoId,
          suggestion: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status === 'INPRG' 
            ? 'Wait for technicians to complete work, or contact supervisor to cancel in Maximo first'
            : 'This PM has been completed/cancelled. Create a new PM if rescheduling is needed.'
        };
      }
    }
    
    // Check 2: Pending sync conflict
    if (pm.shadowRecord?.syncStatus === 'CONFLICT') {
      return {
        valid: false,
        reason: 'SYNC_CONFLICT',
        message: 'Unresolved sync conflict exists. Resolve before editing.',
        suggestion: 'Go to Integration > Conflicts to resolve'
      };
    }
    
    // Check 3: Baseline lock (optional, if locked in current baseline)
    if (pm.lockedInBaseline) {
      return {
        valid: false,
        reason: 'BASELINE_LOCK',
        message: 'PM is locked in published baseline',
        suggestion: 'Unlock in baseline editor or create scenario to test changes'
      };
    }
    
    return { valid: true };
  }
  
  private formatStatus(status: string): string {
    const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
      'INPRG': 'In Progress',
      'COMP': 'Completed',
      'CLOSE': 'Closed',
      'CAN': 'Cancelled'
    };
    return statusLabels[status] || status;
  }
}

interface EditValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  reason?: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' | 'SYNC_CONFLICT' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
  message?: string;
  maximoStatus?: string;
  maximoWonum?: string;
  suggestion?: string;
}
```

**API Integration:**

All PM modification endpoints MUST call this validator:

```typescript
// In PM Schedule API (Part 5)
app.patch('/api/v1/pm-schedule/:id', async (req, res) => {
  const validation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(req.params.id);
  
  if (!validation.valid) {
    return res.status(423).json({  // 423 Locked
      error: validation.reason,
      message: validation.message,
      suggestion: validation.suggestion,
      maximoStatus: validation.maximoStatus
    });
  }
  
  // Proceed with update...
});
```

**Canvas UI Integration:**

When displaying PM blocks, check lock status and show visual indicator:

```typescript
// In Canvas layer (Part 6)
function getPMBlockStyle(pm: PMSchedule): PMBlockStyle {
  if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status === 'INPRG') {
    return {
      cursor: 'not-allowed',
      opacity: 0.7,
      border: '2px dashed #F59E0B',  // Amber dashed = in progress
      draggable: false,
      tooltip: 'In Progress in Maximo - cannot reschedule'
    };
  }
  // ... normal styling
}
```

### 7.4.7 Bidirectional Sync Logic

```typescript
class MaximoSyncService {
  /**
   * Push MMS changes to Maximo
   */
  async pushToMaximo(pmId: string): Promise<SyncResult> {
    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
    
    if (!pm.maximoWoId) {
      // Create new work order
      const wonum = await this.createWorkOrder(pm);
      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
        maximoWoId: wonum, 
        syncStatus: 'SYNCED' 
      });
      return { action: 'CREATED', wonum };
    }
    
    // Update existing work order
    const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
    
    if (maximoWO.status === 'INPRG' || maximoWO.status === 'COMP') {
      // Cannot update in-progress or completed WOs
      return { action: 'SKIPPED', reason: 'WO already in progress' };
    }
    
    // Check for conflicts
    if (new Date(maximoWO.changedate) > pm.shadowRecord.lastSyncAt) {
      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { syncStatus: 'CONFLICT' });
      return { action: 'CONFLICT', maximoData: maximoWO };
    }
    
    // Push update
    await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
      schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
      schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
      location: pm.assignedDepot.code
    });
    
    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
      lastSyncAt: new Date()
    });
    
    return { action: 'UPDATED', wonum: pm.maximoWoId };
  }
  
  /**
   * Pull Maximo changes to MMS
   */
  async pullFromMaximo(): Promise<PullResult> {
    // Get recently modified work orders
    const modifiedSince = await this.getLastPullTimestamp();
    const workOrders = await this.queryMaximoWorkOrders({
      worktype: 'PM',
      changedate: `>${modifiedSince.toISOString()}`
    });
    
    const results: PullResult = {
      processed: 0,
      updated: 0,
      conflicts: 0,
      errors: 0
    };
    
    for (const wo of workOrders) {
      results.processed++;
      
      try {
        const pm = await this.findPMByMaximoId(wo.wonum);
        
        if (!pm) {
          // Work order not managed by MMS
          continue;
        }
        
        // Status change
        if (wo.status !== pm.shadowRecord.maximoData?.status) {
          await this.handleStatusChange(pm, wo);
          results.updated++;
        }
        
        // Actuals recorded
        if (wo.actlabhrs && !pm.actualHours) {
          await this.recordActuals(pm, wo);
          results.updated++;
        }
        
      } catch (error) {
        results.errors++;
        this.logger.error({ wo, error }, 'Pull processing failed');
      }
    }
    
    await this.setLastPullTimestamp(new Date());
    return results;
  }
  
  /**
   * Resolve sync conflict
   */
  async resolveConflict(
    pmId: string, 
    resolution: 'USE_MMS' | 'USE_MAXIMO'
  ): Promise<void> {
    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
    
    if (resolution === 'USE_MMS') {
      // Force push MMS data to Maximo
      await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
        schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
        schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString()
      }, { force: true });
    } else {
      // Apply Maximo data to MMS
      const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
      await this.updatePMFromMaximo(pm.id, maximoWO);
    }
    
    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
      lastSyncAt: new Date()
    });
  }
}
```

### 7.4.7 Inventory Integration

```typescript
interface InventorySnapshotConfig {
  syncFrequency: 'HOURLY' | 'DAILY';
  depots: string[];
  partCategories: string[];  // Filter to maintenance-relevant parts
}

class InventoryIntegration {
  async syncInventory(): Promise<void> {
    for (const depot of this.config.depots) {
      const inventory = await this.maximoClient.queryInventory({
        siteid: 'METRO',
        location: depot,
        category: { $in: this.config.partCategories }
      });
      
      for (const item of inventory) {
        await this.db.upsert(inventoryPositions, {
          depotId: this.getDepotId(depot),
          partNumber: item.itemnum,
          partDescription: item.description,
          quantityOnHand: item.curbal,
          quantityReserved: item.reservedqty,
          quantityOnOrder: item.onorderqty,
          reorderPoint: item.reorder,
          unitCost: item.avgcost,
          lastSyncAt: new Date(),
          sourceSystem: 'MAXIMO'
        });
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### 7.7.2 Report Generation Service

```typescript
class ReportGenerationService {
  async generateReport(
    type: ReportType,
    params: ReportParams
  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
    const template = await this.loadTemplate(type);
    const data = await this.fetchReportData(type, params);
    
    switch (template.format) {
      case 'PDF':
        return this.generatePDF(template, data);
      case 'XLSX':
        return this.generateExcel(template, data);
      default:
        throw new Error(`Unsupported format: ${template.format}`);
    }
  }
  
  private async generatePDF(
    template: ReportTemplate,
    data: any
  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
    // Use Puppeteer for PDF generation
    const html = await this.renderTemplate(template.htmlTemplate, data);
    
    const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ headless: true });
    const page = await browser.newPage();
    await page.setContent(html);
    
    const pdfBuffer = await page.pdf({
      format: 'A4',
      margin: { top: '1cm', right: '1cm', bottom: '1cm', left: '1cm' },
      printBackground: true
    });
    
    await browser.close();
    
    // Store in MinIO
    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.pdf`;
    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, pdfBuffer);
    
    return {
      filename,
      url,
      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
      sizeBytes: pdfBuffer.length
    };
  }
  
  private async generateExcel(
    template: ReportTemplate,
    data: any
  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
    // Use ExcelJS for Excel generation
    const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
    
    for (const sheetDef of template.sheets) {
      const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet(sheetDef.name);
      
      // Add headers
      sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => c.header));
      
      // Add data rows
      const sheetData = data[sheetDef.dataKey];
      for (const row of sheetData) {
        sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => row[c.key]));
      }
      
      // Apply formatting
      this.applyExcelFormatting(sheet, sheetDef);
    }
    
    const buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();
    
    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.xlsx`;
    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, buffer);
    
    return {
      filename,
      url,
      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
      sizeBytes: buffer.length
    };
  }
}
```

### 7.10.2 Circuit Breaker

```typescript
interface CircuitBreakerConfig {
  failureThreshold: number;    // Failures before opening
  successThreshold: number;    // Successes before closing
  timeout: number;             // Time in open state before half-open (ms)
}

class CircuitBreaker {
  private state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN' = 'CLOSED';
  private failures = 0;
  private successes = 0;
  private lastFailureTime: Date | null = null;
  
  async execute<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
    if (this.state === 'OPEN') {
      if (Date.now() - this.lastFailureTime!.getTime() > this.config.timeout) {
        this.state = 'HALF_OPEN';
      } else {
        throw new Error('Circuit breaker is OPEN');
      }
    }
    
    try {
      const result = await fn();
      this.onSuccess();
      return result;
    } catch (error) {
      this.onFailure();
      throw error;
    }
  }
  
  private onSuccess() {
    this.failures = 0;
    if (this.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
      this.successes++;
      if (this.successes >= this.config.successThreshold) {
        this.state = 'CLOSED';
        this.successes = 0;
      }
    }
  }
  
  private onFailure() {
    this.failures++;
    this.lastFailureTime = new Date();
    if (this.failures >= this.config.failureThreshold) {
      this.state = 'OPEN';
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 7.11 Integration Monitoring

```typescript
interface IntegrationHealthCheck {
  name: string;
  type: 'MAXIMO' | 'SMTP' | 'FILE_SYSTEM' | 'DATABASE';
  endpoint?: string;
  checkInterval: number;  // ms
  timeout: number;        // ms
  healthyThreshold: number;
  unhealthyThreshold: number;
}

  },

    unhealthyThreshold: 3
  }
];
```

```typescript
};
```

## Comprehensive MMS System Specifications Overview

### **001-Executive-Summary.md**
Covers the system purpose, project scope, key architectural decisions (14 decision records), risk assessment with mitigation strategies, RFQ compliance matrix, and technology stack summary. Emphasizes the constraint-based nature of the platform and introduces critical ADRs around unavailability events, service demand, route utilization profiles, and PM history assumptions for cold-start scenarios. The document establishes that MMS is a 12-year government contract fleet management system for 128+ trains with strategic and tactical planning capabilities.

### **002-Architecture.md**
Details the high-level system architecture with component responsibilities across frontend (deck.gl Infinite Canvas), backend (Bun + Hono), file watcher service, BullMQ workers, and C++ optimizer binary. Includes critical data flows (daily operational, weekly planning, baseline publishing, scenario comparison), the C++ optimizer stream protocol with NDJSON communication, comprehensive error handling for the optimizer, and the constraint aggregation layer ensuring consistency between strategic (quarterly) and tactical (daily) planning horizons. Establishes foundation for explainability-first design, graceful degradation, full audit trails, and stable long-term planning.

### **003-Database-Schema.md**
Comprehensive PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB schema (v2.1) with 15 domain areas: core fleet management, operational constraints (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, mileage tracking with TimescaleDB hypertable, component tracking, PM management with hierarchy support, track capabilities with PM requirements, PM baseline assumptions for cold-start inference, material management with reservations, man-hour planning, baseline/drift architecture, scenario management with event sourcing, costing, audit trails, and user authentication. Includes canvas-optimized materialized views and database maintenance functions.

### **004-Domain-Services.md** (Document 4 was truncated, but covered)
Contains specification for domain services including PM scheduling, run assignment optimization, man-hour leveling, strategic planning, mileage projection, component tracking, and costing modules. Includes detailed algorithms for PM hierarchy handling, constraint validation, and scenario branching.

### **005-API.md**
REST API specification (v2.0) with 200+ endpoints across 15 sections: authentication, fleet management, mileage tracking, PM scheduling, optimizer control, man-hour management, baseline operations, drift analysis, scenario manipulation, material management, costing, canvas-specific endpoints, WebSocket events with critical PM move handler validating against Maximo status locks, and export functionality. Emphasizes that all PM movements must validate against Maximo to prevent scheduling conflicts with in-progress work.

### **006-User-Interface.md** (Partially read)
Specifies the Infinite Canvas interface with semantic zoom (12-year strategic to daily operational), multiple visualization modes (heatmap, Gantt, detail cards), drag-and-drop PM rescheduling with constraint feedback, man-hour overlay capability, baseline drift comparison views, and scenario analysis interface. Uses deck.gl for WebGL rendering at scale with progressive enhancement.

### **007-Integration.md**
Integration specifications (v2.0) for external systems covering: mileage CSV ingestion with validation rules, Maximo integration via shadow records and REST API with bidirectional sync and critical status-based edit locking (prevents rescheduling in-progress work orders), OCC integration for run assignment export, email notification system with 7 notification types, report generation (PDF/Excel), SSO support for future, API key authentication, data export formats, retry logic with circuit breaker pattern, and integration monitoring with health checks and metrics.

### **008-Operations-Deployment.md**
Operations & deployment specification (v2.0) covering production Kubernetes topology, container images (frontend, backend, worker, optimizer, file-watcher), Dockerfile examples, K8s manifests with HPA configuration, PostgreSQL tuning and backup strategy (daily full backup + WAL archiving with 7-day retention and PITR), migration strategy using Drizzle ORM with advisory locking, Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards, alerting rules, structured logging, network policies, secrets management, pod security standards, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, and disaster recovery procedures with RTO/RPO targets (1-hour RTO, 5-minute RPO).

### **009-Demo-Application.md**
Demo application specification (v1.0) for win-the-bid client demonstration with strategic goals around showing financial impact of bow-wave risk through semantic zoom from 10-year heatmap to daily operations. Includes CSV import for fleet roster and PM history, PM projection engine with cold-start handling and PM hierarchy (C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3), "Chaos Monkey" tool to artificially create bow-waves if client data is well-maintained, Zustand state management, deck.gl-only rendering (no Konva to avoid jitter), drag-and-drop with constraint validation, and comprehensive acceptance criteria. Development timeline: 10 days for single senior frontend engineer.

---

## Critical System Characteristics

**Architecture Decisions (14 ADRs):**
- TypeScript-first with C++ native optimizer (OR-Tools CP-SAT)
- Dual-horizon planning (strategic quarterly + tactical daily)
- Soft constraints with explainability
- Material constraints integrated into optimizer
- Baseline/drift architecture for stability
- Scenario-based simulation with event sourcing
- Unavailability event integration (Maximo awareness)
- Service demand as hard constraint
- Route-based utilization profiles
- PM history assumptions for cold-start

**Key Integrations:**
- Maximo (shadow records, status-based locking, bidirectional sync)
- Inventory system (daily snapshots)
- OCC (run assignment export)
- Email (notifications)
- CSV (mileage ingestion)

**Technology Stack:**
- Frontend: React 18, deck.gl, BlueprintJS v6, Zustand, Vite
- Backend: Bun 1.1+, Hono, Drizzle ORM, TypeScript
- Database: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
- Cache/Queue: Redis 7.x, BullMQ
- Optimizer: C++ (OR-Tools), NDJSON stream protocol
- Observability: OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana
- Deployment: Docker Compose (dev), Kubernetes (prod)

**Critical Constraints:**
- 12-year government contract compliance requiring full audit trails
- Maximo status must lock PMs during in-progress work
- Service demand (min fleet availability) is a hard constraint
- Route utilization profiles required for mileage projection accuracy
- PM history assumptions needed for data migration edge cases

This is a production-grade, constraint-based fleet management system designed for complex optimization at scale with deep operator trust through explainability and stability-focused design patterns."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ab06d20 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c8f9eabf-07e5-4b35-bc68-02cf90b768ab","uuid":"879a235b-95a7-4ca8-8f88-39851af7ad97","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Read all spec files in the specs/ directory. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LWhF6XvQmNefYURMszTU7b","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2→# Part 3: Database Schema
     3→
     4→**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5→**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6→**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 3.1 Schema Overview
    11→
    12→The database schema is organized into the following domains:
    13→
    14→| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
    15→|--------|--------|---------|
    16→| Core Fleet | `fleets`, `trains`, `depots`, `routes` | Asset master data |
    17→| **Operational Constraints** | `service_demand`, `route_utilization_profiles`, `train_route_compatibility` | **Operational requirements and constraints** |
    18→| **Unavailability** | `unavailability_events` | **Corrective maintenance and incident tracking** |
    19→| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
    20→| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
    21→| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
    22→| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
    23→| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
    24→| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
    25→| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
    26→| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
    27→| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
    28→| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
    29→| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
    30→| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |
    31→
    32→> **v2.1 Changes:** Added operational constraints domain (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, track capabilities with PM requirements, and data migration assumptions tables.
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables
    37→
    38→```sql
    39→-- Enable required extensions
    40→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
    41→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
    42→
    43→-- Enumerated types
    44→CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
    45→CREATE TYPE pm_status AS ENUM ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED');
    46→CREATE TYPE track_type AS ENUM ('LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING');
    47→CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY');
    48→CREATE TYPE adjustment_status AS ENUM ('PROPOSED', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'APPLIED');
    49→CREATE TYPE baseline_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PUBLISHED', 'SUPERSEDED', 'ARCHIVED');
    50→CREATE TYPE scenario_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'PROMOTED', 'DISCARDED', 'EXPIRED');
    51→CREATE TYPE material_criticality AS ENUM ('BLOCKING', 'DEFERRABLE', 'CONSUMABLE');
    52→CREATE TYPE reservation_type AS ENUM ('SOFT', 'HARD');
    53→
    54→-- NEW: v2.1 enum types
    55→CREATE TYPE day_type AS ENUM ('WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY');
    56→CREATE TYPE unavailability_type AS ENUM ('CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', 'COLLISION', 'VANDALISM', 'EXTERNAL', 'PLANNED_OUTAGE');
    57→CREATE TYPE unavailability_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED');
    58→CREATE TYPE track_capability AS ENUM ('PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE');
    59→CREATE TYPE constraint_type AS ENUM ('SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', 'PLATFORM_LENGTH', 'MAX_SPEED', 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE');
    60→CREATE TYPE assumption_type AS ENUM ('INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING', 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE', 'IMPORTED', 'NO_HISTORY');
    61→CREATE TYPE confidence_level AS ENUM ('HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
    62→
    63→-- Depots
    64→CREATE TABLE depots (
    65→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    66→    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    67→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    68→    location VARCHAR(200),
    69→    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Asia/Singapore',
    70→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    71→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    72→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    73→);
    74→
    75→-- Fleets (train types/manufacturers)
    76→CREATE TABLE fleets (
    77→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    78→    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'KS', 'ALSTOM'
    79→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    80→    manufacturer VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    81→    model VARCHAR(100),
    82→    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    83→    cars_per_train INTEGER DEFAULT 6,
    84→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    85→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    86→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    87→);
    88→
    89→-- Trains
    90→CREATE TABLE trains (
    91→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    92→    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    93→    train_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    94→    commissioning_date DATE,
    95→    current_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    96→    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    97→    fitness_notes TEXT,
    98→    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    99→    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   100→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   101→    
   102→    -- For mileage balancing
   103→    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
   104→    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   105→        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
   106→    
   107→    -- Canvas display properties
   108→    display_color VARCHAR(7),
   109→    display_order INTEGER,
   110→    
   111→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   112→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   113→);
   114→
   115→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
   116→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
   117→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);
   118→
   119→-- Routes
   120→CREATE TABLE routes (
   121→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   122→    route_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   123→    route_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   124→    line_id VARCHAR(20),
   125→    expected_km_per_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
   126→    required_trains INTEGER NOT NULL,
   127→    
   128→    -- NEW v2.1: Route characteristics for compatibility matching
   129→    default_daily_km INTEGER,                        -- Default mileage for projection
   130→    signaling_system VARCHAR(30),                    -- 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'
   131→    power_system VARCHAR(30),                        -- 'THIRD_RAIL_750V', 'OVERHEAD_25KV', 'OVERHEAD_1500V'
   132→    tunnel_clearance_mm INTEGER,                     -- Maximum vehicle width allowed
   133→    platform_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),                  -- Platform length constraint
   134→    max_axle_load_tonnes DECIMAL(4,2),               -- Track weight limit
   135→    
   136→    -- Restrictions
   137→    allowed_fleet_ids UUID[],
   138→    requires_ato BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   139→    min_cars INTEGER,
   140→    max_cars INTEGER,
   141→    
   142→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   143→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   144→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   145→);
   146→
   147→-- Tracks (maintenance bays)
   148→CREATE TABLE tracks (
   149→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   150→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   151→    track_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
   152→    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
   153→    max_train_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),
   154→    has_overhead_crane BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   155→    has_pit_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   156→    
   157→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   158→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   159→    
   160→    UNIQUE(depot_id, track_number)
   161→);
   162→
   163→CREATE INDEX idx_tracks_depot_type ON tracks(depot_id, track_type) WHERE active = TRUE;
   164→```
   165→
   166→---
   167→
   168→## 3.2.1 Service Demand Tables (NEW v2.1)
   169→
   170→Service demand defines the minimum number of trains required for passenger operations by day type. This is a **hard constraint** for the optimizer - it cannot schedule maintenance that would violate availability targets.
   171→
   172→```sql
   173→-- Service demand by fleet and day type
   174→CREATE TABLE service_demand (
   175→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   176→    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
   177→    effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
   178→    day_type day_type NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY'
   179→    
   180→    -- Minimum availability requirements
   181→    min_trains_required INTEGER NOT NULL,
   182→    peak_am_requirement INTEGER,       -- 6:00-9:00 requirement
   183→    midday_requirement INTEGER,        -- 9:00-16:00 requirement  
   184→    peak_pm_requirement INTEGER,       -- 16:00-19:00 requirement
   185→    evening_requirement INTEGER,       -- 19:00-23:00 requirement
   186→    standby_buffer INTEGER DEFAULT 2,  -- Additional trains for contingency
   187→    
   188→    -- Metadata
   189→    source VARCHAR(50),                -- 'OPERATIONS', 'CONTRACT', 'MANUAL'
   190→    notes TEXT,
   191→    
   192→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   193→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   194→    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   195→    
   196→    UNIQUE(fleet_id, effective_date, day_type)
   197→);
   198→
   199→CREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_fleet ON service_demand(fleet_id, effective_date DESC);
   200→CREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_lookup ON service_demand(fleet_id, day_type, effective_date DESC);
   201→
   202→-- Example data:
   203→-- INSERT INTO service_demand (fleet_id, effective_date, day_type, min_trains_required, peak_am_requirement, standby_buffer)
   204→-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'WEEKDAY', 45, 52, 3);
   205→-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SATURDAY', 32, 35, 2);
   206→-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SUNDAY', 28, 30, 2);
   207→```
   208→
   209→---
   210→
   211→## 3.2.2 Route Utilization Profiles (NEW v2.1)
   212→
   213→Route utilization profiles capture the expected daily mileage by route, enabling accurate long-term projections. Without this, mileage projection uses fleet-wide averages which can be off by 60,000+ km over 12 years.
   214→
   215→```sql
   216→-- Route-specific utilization profiles
   217→CREATE TABLE route_utilization_profiles (
   218→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   219→    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
   220→    effective_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   221→    
   222→    -- Daily mileage by day type (km)
   223→    weekday_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   224→    saturday_km INTEGER,
   225→    sunday_km INTEGER,
   226→    holiday_km INTEGER,
   227→    
   228→    -- Seasonal adjustments
   229→    peak_season_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,  -- e.g., 1.15 for CNY
   230→    off_peak_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
   231→    
   232→    -- Service characteristics
   233→    service_hours_per_day DECIMAL(4,1),               -- Operating hours
   234→    round_trip_km DECIMAL(6,2),                       -- Single round trip distance
   235→    trips_per_day INTEGER,                            -- Expected service runs
   236→    
   237→    -- Metadata
   238→    source VARCHAR(50),                               -- 'MEASURED', 'ESTIMATED', 'CONTRACT'
   239→    confidence confidence_level DEFAULT 'MEDIUM',
   240→    notes TEXT,
   241→    
   242→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   243→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   244→    
   245→    UNIQUE(route_id, effective_date)
   246→);
   247→
   248→CREATE INDEX idx_route_util_route ON route_utilization_profiles(route_id, effective_date DESC);
   249→
   250→-- Example data:
   251→-- North-South Line: High utilization (350 km/day weekday)
   252→-- INSERT INTO route_utilization_profiles (route_id, effective_date, weekday_km, saturday_km, sunday_km, confidence)
   253→-- VALUES ('route-nsl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 350, 280, 240, 'HIGH');
   254→-- East-West Line: Medium utilization (220 km/day weekday)
   255→-- VALUES ('route-ewl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 220, 180, 150, 'HIGH');
   256→```
   257→
   258→---
   259→
   260→## 3.2.3 Unavailability Events (NEW v2.1)
   261→
   262→Unavailability events track trains that cannot be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other blocking factors. This is a **critical integration point** with Maximo CMMS.
   263→
   264→```sql
   265→-- Unavailability events (corrective maintenance, incidents, etc.)
   266→CREATE TABLE unavailability_events (
   267→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   268→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   269→    
   270→    -- Event classification
   271→    event_type unavailability_type NOT NULL,  -- 'CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', etc.
   272→    status unavailability_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
   273→    restriction_level fitness_status NOT NULL, -- Uses existing ENUM: 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
   274→    
   275→    -- Timing
   276→    reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   277→    estimated_repair_hours INTEGER,
   278→    estimated_return_date DATE,
   279→    actual_return_date DATE,
   280→    
   281→    -- External system linkage
   282→    source_system VARCHAR(30),        -- 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
   283→    external_reference VARCHAR(50),   -- Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
   284→    external_status VARCHAR(30),      -- Maximo status (e.g., 'INPRG', 'WAPPR')
   285→    
   286→    -- Description
   287→    description TEXT,
   288→    failure_code VARCHAR(30),         -- Standard failure classification
   289→    affected_system VARCHAR(50),      -- 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', 'DOORS', 'HVAC', etc.
   290→    
   291→    -- Scheduling impact flags
   292→    blocks_pm_scheduling BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   293→    blocks_run_assignment BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   294→    
   295→    -- Resolution
   296→    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   297→    resolved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   298→    resolution_notes TEXT,
   299→    
   300→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   301→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   302→    
   303→    -- Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
   304→    CONSTRAINT chk_resolved_status CHECK (
   305→        (status = 'ACTIVE' AND resolved_at IS NULL) OR
   306→        (status = 'RESOLVED' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL)
   307→    )
   308→);
   309→
   310→-- Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
   311→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_active ON unavailability_events(train_id, status) 
   312→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   313→-- Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
   314→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_return ON unavailability_events(estimated_return_date) 
   315→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   316→-- Maximo sync: Find by external reference
   317→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_external ON unavailability_events(source_system, external_reference);
   318→-- Audit: Find by type and date range
   319→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_audit ON unavailability_events(event_type, reported_at DESC);
   320→
   321→-- Trigger to update train fitness_status when unavailability changes
   322→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
   323→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   324→BEGIN
   325→    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' OR TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
   326→        IF NEW.status = 'ACTIVE' THEN
   327→            UPDATE trains 
   328→            SET fitness_status = NEW.restriction_level,
   329→                fitness_notes = COALESCE(NEW.description, 'Unavailability event: ' || NEW.event_type),
   330→                updated_at = NOW()
   331→            WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   332→        ELSIF NEW.status = 'RESOLVED' THEN
   333→            -- Only reset to FIT if no other active unavailability exists
   334→            IF NOT EXISTS (
   335→                SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events 
   336→                WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id 
   337→                AND status = 'ACTIVE' 
   338→                AND id != NEW.id
   339→            ) THEN
   340→                UPDATE trains 
   341→                SET fitness_status = 'FIT',
   342→                    fitness_notes = NULL,
   343→                    updated_at = NOW()
   344→                WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   345→            END IF;
   346→        END IF;
   347→    END IF;
   348→    RETURN NEW;
   349→END;
   350→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   351→
   352→CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
   353→AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON unavailability_events
   354→FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→## 3.2.4 Train-Route Compatibility (NEW v2.1)
   360→
   361→Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the optimizer from assigning trains to incompatible routes.
   362→
   363→```sql
   364→-- Train-route compatibility matrix
   365→CREATE TABLE train_route_compatibility (
   366→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   367→    
   368→    -- Can be at train or fleet level
   369→    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   370→    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
   371→    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
   372→    
   373→    -- Compatibility determination
   374→    is_compatible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
   375→    constraint_type constraint_type,   -- 'SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', etc.
   376→    restriction_reason TEXT,           -- Human-readable explanation
   377→    
   378→    -- Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
   379→    effective_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   380→    expiry_date DATE,                  -- NULL = permanent
   381→    
   382→    -- Metadata
   383→    verified_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   384→    verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   385→    notes TEXT,
   386→    
   387→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   388→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   389→    
   390→    -- Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
   391→    CONSTRAINT chk_train_or_fleet CHECK (
   392→        (train_id IS NOT NULL AND fleet_id IS NULL) OR
   393→        (train_id IS NULL AND fleet_id IS NOT NULL)
   394→    )
   395→);
   396→
   397→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_route ON train_route_compatibility(route_id, is_compatible);
   398→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_train ON train_route_compatibility(train_id, is_compatible) 
   399→    WHERE train_id IS NOT NULL;
   400→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_fleet ON train_route_compatibility(fleet_id, is_compatible) 
   401→    WHERE fleet_id IS NOT NULL;
   402→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_active ON train_route_compatibility(effective_date, expiry_date)
   403→    WHERE is_compatible = FALSE;
   404→
   405→-- Example data:
   406→-- Fleet KS cannot run on Line 4 (no CBTC equipment)
   407→-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (fleet_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason)
   408→-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', 'route-line4-uuid', FALSE, 'SIGNALING', 'KS fleet lacks CBTC - only ATC equipped');
   409→
   410→-- Train 1042 temporarily restricted from NEL (oversized pantograph)
   411→-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (train_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason, effective_date, expiry_date)
   412→-- VALUES ('train-1042-uuid', 'route-nel-uuid', FALSE, 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE', 'Temporary - pantograph modification pending', '2026-01-01', '2026-03-31');
   413→```
   414→
   415→---
   416→
   417→## 3.2.5 Track Capabilities (NEW v2.1)
   418→
   419→Defines the specific equipment and features available on each maintenance track. Enables constraint-based scheduling that matches PM requirements to track capabilities.
   420→
   421→```sql
   422→-- Track capability definitions
   423→CREATE TABLE track_capabilities (
   424→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   425→    track_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tracks(id),
   426→    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
   427→    -- 'PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 
   428→    -- 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE'
   429→    
   430→    -- Capacity limits for this capability
   431→    capacity_limit JSONB,  -- e.g., {"max_axle_load_tonnes": 17, "max_lift_tonnes": 25}
   432→    
   433→    -- Availability
   434→    is_operational BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   435→    maintenance_schedule TEXT,         -- e.g., "Unavailable Tuesdays 06:00-10:00"
   436→    
   437→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   438→    
   439→    UNIQUE(track_id, capability)
   440→);
   441→
   442→CREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_track ON track_capabilities(track_id);
   443→CREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_type ON track_capabilities(capability) WHERE is_operational = TRUE;
   444→
   445→-- PM type capability requirements
   446→CREATE TABLE pm_capability_requirements (
   447→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   448→    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   449→    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
   450→    is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,  -- FALSE = preferred but not required
   451→    
   452→    notes TEXT,                         -- Explanation of why this capability is needed
   453→    
   454→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   455→    
   456→    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, capability)
   457→);
   458→
   459→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_pm ON pm_capability_requirements(pm_type_id);
   460→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_cap ON pm_capability_requirements(capability);
   461→
   462→-- Example data:
   463→-- Track T1 at Bishan has pit, crane, and lifting jacks
   464→-- INSERT INTO track_capabilities (track_id, capability)
   465→-- VALUES ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'PIT'),
   466→--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'CRANE'),
   467→--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS');
   468→
   469→-- C4 overhaul requires pit and lifting jacks (mandatory), crane (preferred)
   470→-- INSERT INTO pm_capability_requirements (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
   471→-- VALUES ('pm-c4-uuid', 'PIT', TRUE),
   472→--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS', TRUE),
   473→--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'CRANE', FALSE);
   474→```

   477→
   478→## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)
   479→
   480→Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.
   481→
   482→```sql
   483→-- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
   484→CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
   485→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   486→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   487→    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   488→    
   489→    -- Assumption details
   490→    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
   491→    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
   492→    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
   493→    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
   494→    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
   495→    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
   496→    
   497→    -- Assumed baseline values
   498→    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
   499→    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
   500→    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
   501→    
   502→    -- Inference source documentation
   503→    inference_source TEXT,             -- Explanation of how assumption was derived
   504→    inference_data JSONB,              -- Supporting data (e.g., higher PM dates)
   505→    
   506→    -- Review workflow
   507→    requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   508→    reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   509→    reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   510→    review_notes TEXT,
   511→    
   512→    -- When applied
   513→    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   514→    applied_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   515→    
   516→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   517→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   518→    
   519→    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
   520→);
   521→
   522→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
   523→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
   524→    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
   525→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
   526→    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
   527→
   528→-- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
   529→-- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
   530→--    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
   531→-- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
   532→--    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
   533→-- 3. If no data at all:
   534→--    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
   535→```

   539→## 3.3 Mileage Tracking Tables
   540→
   541→```sql
   542→-- Meter events (odometer replacements, resets, swaps)
   543→CREATE TABLE meter_events (
   544→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   545→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   546→    event_date DATE NOT NULL,
   547→    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'REPLACEMENT', 'RESET', 'CALIBRATION', 'SWAP'
   548→    
   549→    old_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
   550→    new_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
   551→    old_meter_reading INTEGER,
   552→    new_meter_reading INTEGER,
   553→    adjustment_km INTEGER,  -- Cumulative mileage adjustment to apply
   554→    
   555→    reason TEXT,
   556→    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   557→    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   558→    
   559→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   560→    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
   561→);
   562→
   563→CREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);
   564→
   565→-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
   566→CREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
   567→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   568→    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
   569→    
   570→    -- Raw and adjusted values
   571→    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
   572→    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
   573→    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
   574→    
   575→    -- Validation
   576→    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
   577→    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
   578→    anomaly_notes TEXT,
   579→    
   580→    -- Source tracking
   581→    source_file VARCHAR(255),
   582→    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
   583→    
   584→    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   585→    
   586→    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
   587→);
   588→
   589→-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
   590→SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
   591→    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
   592→    if_not_exists => TRUE
   593→);
   594→
   595→-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
   596→SELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');
   597→
   598→-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
   599→SELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');
   600→
   601→-- Indexes for common queries
   602→CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
   603→CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
   604→    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';
   605→
   606→-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
   607→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
   608→WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
   609→SELECT 
   610→    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
   611→    t.fleet_id,
   612→    COUNT(*) as train_count,
   613→    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
   614→    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
   615→    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
   616→    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
   617→    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
   618→FROM mileage_readings mr
   619→JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
   620→WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
   621→GROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
   622→WITH NO DATA;
   623→
   624→SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
   625→    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
   626→    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
   627→    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
   628→);
   629→```

   636→-- Train components (bogies, motors, pantographs, etc.)

   668→CREATE TABLE component_installations (

   690→
   691→-- Component-based mileage adjustments

   693→CREATE TABLE component_pm_tracking (

   705→    UNIQUE(component_id, pm_type_id)
   706→);
